question.text: EURINR spot is 91.00. India's 6-month rate is 7% p.a. and Eurozone's 6-month rate is 3.5% p.a. What is the 6-month EURINR forward price?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** F = S × (1 + RQC × t) / (1 + RBC × t) = 91.00 × (1 + 0.07 × 0.5) / (1 + 0.035 × 0.5) = 91.00 × 1.035 / 1.0175 = 92.5651 ≈ 92.57. INR is the quoting currency (RQC = 7%), EUR is the base currency (RBC = 3.5%). **The Trap:** Swapping the rates — using 3.5% as RQC gives 89.43, which implies EUR depreciating (wrong when Indian rates are higher). **Cross-Exam Context:** IRP applies to all currency pairs, not just USDINR — tested in Series I and XIII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Since Indian rates exceed Eurozone rates, EURINR futures trade at a premium — it costs more INR to buy EUR in the future.
